description: Rebuild popsindustrial.com on Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4 + shadcn as apps/pops-website/ inside this repo. Content sourced from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/. Design tokens from docs/design/popsindustrial-design-principles.md. Comprehensive PRD at docs/prd/POPS-WEBSITE-REBUILD.md.

userStories.title: Add apps/pops-website pnpm workspace + Next.js 16 scaffold
userStories.description: As a builder, I need apps/pops-website/ to exist as a pnpm workspace package with Next.js 16 App Router so all subsequent stories have a place to land.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm-workspace.yaml at repo root includes 'apps/*' under packages
apps/pops-website/package.json exists with name 'pops-website', private: true, scripts: dev/build/start/type-check/lint
apps/pops-website/next.config.ts exists with images.formats including 'image/avif' and 'image/webp'
apps/pops-website/tsconfig.json extends a strict config (no any allowed)
apps/pops-website/app/layout.tsx and apps/pops-website/app/page.tsx exist (placeholder content OK)
Running 'pnpm --filter pops-website build' from repo root succeeds
Running 'pnpm --filter pops-website type-check' from repo root succeeds
Existing src/ at repo root is unchanged (verify with git diff --stat)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read docs/prd/POPS-WEBSITE-REBUILD.md §2 for the target layout. Don't touch src/ — that's the SaaS app and is out of scope. Match the Next.js 16 version pinned in root package.json.

userStories.title: Add Tailwind v4 + design tokens to apps/pops-website/app/globals.css
userStories.description: As a designer, I need every color, type, radius, and elevation token from the design principles available as CSS variables and Tailwind v4 utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/app/globals.css contains an @import 'tailwindcss' directive

globals.css contains an @theme block with the EXACT token set from docs/design/popsindustrial-design-principles.md §10 (color-pops-yellow-100/300/500/600, color-pops-tan-400, color-ink-50 through 900, color-paper, semantic 100/500 pairs for success/warn/danger/info, font-display/text/mono, radius-none/sm/md/lg/full, shadow-1/2/3/4)
globals.css sets html { background: var(--color-ink-800); color: var(--color-ink-100); }
globals.css adds @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; } }
apps/pops-website/postcss.config.mjs configures @tailwindcss/postcss
apps/pops-website/package.json devDependencies include tailwindcss@^4 and @tailwindcss/postcss@^4
Build still succeeds: 'pnpm --filter pops-website build'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy the @theme block VERBATIM from §10 of the design principles. The SaaS at src/ uses different tokens — don't cross-pollinate. No tailwind.config.ts — Tailwind v4 is CSS-first per CLAUDE.md.

userStories.title: Wire next/font Archivo Black + Inter via app/layout.tsx
userStories.description: As a designer, I need the display font (Archivo Black) and text font (Inter) loaded via next/font so they self-host without FOUT and expose --font-display and --font-text CSS variables.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/app/layout.tsx imports Archivo_Black and Inter from 'next/font/google'
Archivo_Black config: weight: '400', subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-display', display: 'swap'
Inter config: subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-text', display: 'swap'
<html> element gets className combining both font.variable values
<body> uses font-text by default (via Tailwind utility or inline style referencing var(--font-text))
Build succeeds and the network tab shows fonts loaded from /_next/static (self-hosted, not Google CDN)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: render apps/pops-website dev server, confirm h1 uses Archivo Black and body uses Inter
userStories.notes: Per design principles §4.4 — only 'latin' subset for Wave 1 (English-only). Don't add latin-ext. JetBrains Mono is mentioned in principles but is for the SaaS app, not this marketing site — skip it here.

userStories.title: Copy scraped images and PDFs into apps/pops-website/public/
userStories.description: As a developer, I need every image and PDF from the scrape available as static assets so subsequent page stories can reference them by path.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/public/images/ contains all 194 image files from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/assets/images/
apps/pops-website/public/pdfs/ contains all 4 PDF files from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/assets/pdfs/
apps/pops-website/public/favicon.ico exists (convert from cropped-favicon.png)
apps/pops-website/public/site-globals.json copied from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/_raw/site-globals.json (for build-time content reference, NOT served to client)
Wait, correct that — site-globals.json should live at apps/pops-website/content/site-globals.json (NOT in public/) so it's a server-side import
apps/pops-website/content/site-globals.json copied from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/_raw/site-globals.json
apps/pops-website/.gitignore does NOT exclude public/ or content/
Build succeeds with all assets present
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are static assets. Use 'cp -R' from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/assets/{images,pdfs}/ to apps/pops-website/public/{images,pdfs}/. Ignore the AC line that contains 'Wait, correct that' — the corrected location (content/ not public/) for site-globals.json is the actual rule.

userStories.title: Build content/company.ts single-source-of-truth file
userStories.description: As a developer, I need company facts (name, addresses, phone, fax, emails, hours, taglines) in one TypeScript module so the footer, contact page, and JSON-LD all read from the same source.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/content/company.ts exists and exports a const company object

Object includes: name, founded (1972), generations (4), mainOffice {street, city, state, zip}, shippingReceiving {street, city, state, zip}, phone, fax, emails {info, invoices}, hours, tagline, taglineSecondary, services (array of 5)
mainOffice.zip is '33811'
shippingReceiving.zip is '33811' (NOT 33813 — see open question §12 in PRD)
Object is exported with 'as const' assertion for literal types
A type Company = typeof company is exported
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source values from docs/prd/POPS-WEBSITE-REBUILD.md §7. The 33813 vs 33811 ZIP discrepancy is flagged as open question §12.1 — until client confirms, lock 33811. Add a TODO comment on the shipping zip pointing to that open question.

userStories.title: Build Container and Section layout primitives
userStories.description: As a developer, I need <Container> and <Section> components that enforce consistent max-width, padding, and section rhythm across every page.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/layout/container.tsx exists, exports Container with className passthrough
Container caps width at 1280px (max-w-[1280px]) with horizontal padding via Tailwind responsive utilities
apps/pops-website/components/layout/section.tsx exists, exports Section taking { tone?: 'dark' | 'light', children, className? }
Section dark uses bg-ink-800; light uses bg-paper
Section provides vertical padding from spacing scale (py-16 md:py-24 mapping to --space-16 / --space-24)
Both components use forwardRef and accept all standard HTML element props
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Spacing scale is in design principles §5.1 (4px base, --space-1 through --space-32). These primitives are used by every subsequent page story — keep them minimal and stable.

userStories.title: Build EyebrowLabel component
userStories.description: As a designer, I need an uppercase tracked micro-label component for use above every section heading per design principles §6.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/marketing/eyebrow.tsx exports EyebrowLabel taking { children, tone?: 'yellow' | 'ink' }
Default tone is 'yellow' rendering text-pops-yellow-500
Style: text-xs (12px), uppercase, tracking +0.04em, font-weight 600 (Inter), inline-block

Renders as

by default with appropriate ARIA-friendly markup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Eyebrow labels appear above every H2 in the rebuild — see design principles §6.2 hero pattern and §6.3 service cards.

userStories.title: Build Button component with 4 variants
userStories.description: As a designer, I need primary/secondary/ghost/destructive button variants per design principles §6.1 to fix the live site's single-button problem.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/ui/button.tsx exports Button using class-variance-authority (cva)
Variants: 'primary' (bg-pops-yellow-500 text-ink-900), 'secondary' (transparent border-1 currentColor), 'ghost' (transparent text-ink-400), 'destructive' (bg-danger-500 text-paper)
Sizes: 'default' (h-11 = 44px), 'compact' (h-9 = 36px)
Padding: px-6 py-3
Radius: rounded-sm (--radius-sm = 2px)
Font: font-text font-semibold text-sm, NOT uppercase
Focus ring: ring-2 ring-pops-yellow-300 ring-offset-2 (matches --shadow-focus)
Disabled state: opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed
Loading state: replaces children with a 16px spinner; preserves button width
Uses Radix Slot when asChild prop is true (for use as a Link wrapper)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: render a test page with one of each variant; tab through to confirm focus ring visible
userStories.notes: Per design principles §6.1 — 44px default height is critical (touch target). Don't make buttons uppercase — that's reserved for micro-labels and the brand wordmark. Install shadcn button primitive via 'pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add button' if helpful, then customize per the variants here.

userStories.title: Build Card component
userStories.description: As a designer, I need a Card primitive for service tiles, leadership cards, and certification cards.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/ui/card.tsx exports Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter
Card uses bg-ink-700 by default, with 1px border in border-ink-600 (per design principles §5.3 — shadow alone fails on dark surfaces)
Card radius is rounded-sm (--radius-sm)
Card supports hover:shadow-3 lift on interactive cards (when role='link' or wrapping a Link)
Padding: p-6 default
Light variant (prop tone='light') uses bg-paper text-ink-800 with border-ink-200
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Install shadcn card primitive then customize. Cards are used in service tiles (US-016), leadership grid (US-020), and certifications (US-023).

userStories.title: Build form input primitives (Input, Textarea, Label)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need accessible form input primitives that match the design principles §6.4 form spec.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/forms/input.tsx exports Input — h-11 (44px), bg-ink-700, border-1 border-ink-600, rounded-sm, px-4, text-base font-text
apps/pops-website/components/forms/textarea.tsx exports Textarea with same surface styling, min-h-[120px]
apps/pops-website/components/forms/label.tsx exports Label — text-sm font-medium block mb-2
Focus state on Input/Textarea: border-pops-yellow-500 + ring-2 ring-pops-yellow-300
Error state (data-error or aria-invalid): border-danger-500
All three components use forwardRef and pass through HTML attributes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill on a test page
userStories.notes: Per design principles §6.4 — labels above inputs, NEVER floating placeholders for B2B forms (manufacturers fill on tablets). Install shadcn input/textarea/label primitives then customize.

userStories.title: Build FormField wrapper with error helper text
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a FormField that wraps a label, input, and helper/error text and wires aria-describedby for accessibility.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/forms/form-field.tsx exports FormField
Props: { label: string, name: string, required?: boolean, error?: string, helperText?: string, children: ReactNode }
Renders

Wraps children in a div, then renders helper text or error message below in

with id={`${name}-help`}
Sets aria-describedby on the wrapped input via cloneElement to point to the helper id
Error message uses text-danger-500
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Per design principles §6.4 + §9 — errors must be programmatically described. Use React.cloneElement on children to inject id and aria-describedby.

userStories.title: Build Checkbox component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an accessible Checkbox primitive for forms that accept a checkbox (e.g. guest safety acknowledgment on /check-in).
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/forms/checkbox.tsx exports Checkbox
Uses Radix UI Checkbox primitive (@radix-ui/react-checkbox)
Visible 18×18 box with 2px border, rounded-sm
Checked state: bg-pops-yellow-500 with ink-900 checkmark icon
Focus ring: ring-2 ring-pops-yellow-300
Tap target ≥ 44×44 via padding around the visual box
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: keyboard space toggles, screen-reader announces state
userStories.notes: Add @radix-ui/react-checkbox to apps/pops-website/package.json. The 44×44 tap target is non-negotiable per design principles §9.

userStories.title: Build Footer component from content/site-globals.json
userStories.description: As a designer, I need the global Footer with three nav columns, both addresses, phone tel:, fax, both emails, hours, and a 'Get a Quote' primary CTA — exactly matching the live site's footer structure.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/layout/footer.tsx exports <Footer />
Three columns rendering from content/site-globals.json#footer.navigation_columns: 'Doing business with Pop's Industrial', 'Services', 'Visiting Us'
Logo (use /images/Pops-no-border.png) in column 1
Main Office and Shipping & Receiving addresses rendered with <address> element
Phone uses <a href='tel:+18636447473'>; fax displays as text
Both emails use <a href='mailto:...'>
Primary CTA Button (variant='primary') 'GET A QUOTE' linking to /request-a-quote
Footer uses bg-ink-900 text-ink-100
Mobile: stacks columns vertically; desktop: 3-column grid
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: footer renders on a test page; tap targets ≥44px; tab order is logical
userStories.notes: Source ALL link hrefs from content/site-globals.json — don't retype. Source addresses/phone/emails from content/company.ts. Use logo /images/Pops-no-border.png (canonical wordmark per PRD §12.3).

userStories.title: Build Header component (HOME link + brand mark)
userStories.description: As a designer, I need a minimal Header — the live site only has a HOME link in the nav. Replicate that, but with the canonical wordmark and clean typography.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/layout/header.tsx exports <Header />
Sticky header (position sticky top-0 z-50) bg-ink-900/95 backdrop-blur
Left: <Link href='/'> showing logo /images/Pops-no-border.png at 40px height + text 'POP'S INDUSTRIAL COATINGS' in font-display tracking tight
Right: <Link href='/'> labeled 'HOME' in text-sm uppercase font-text font-medium (matches live-site behavior per site-globals.json#header.note)
Skip-to-content link as the first focusable element: Skip to content
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tab order starts with skip-link; logo and HOME both navigate to /
userStories.notes: Per site-globals.json#header.note — the live site has NO main nav menu. Don't invent one in this story. Future iterations can add a fuller nav once content owners decide. Skip-to-content is required by design principles §9.

userStories.title: Build Hero component (rebuilt per design principles §6.2)
userStories.description: As a designer, I need a Hero that fixes the live site's content-light hero — eyebrow, display heading, lede, dual CTA, dark-overlay background photo.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/marketing/hero.tsx exports <Hero {...props} />
Props: { eyebrow: string, heading: string, lede?: string, primaryCta: { label: string, href: string }, secondaryCta?: { label: string, href: string }, backgroundImage: string }
Layout: full-width, min-h-[560px] desktop / min-h-[480px] mobile
Background: <Image fill> with priority + placeholder='blur' OR static optimized; overlay div bg-ink-900/60
Content: max-w-[1280px] mx-auto px-6, vertically centered

Renders on top, then

, then

, then a button row with primary + secondary
On mobile: heading scales from 56px to 36px; CTAs stack
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: hero renders on a placeholder home page; tab order: skip-link → primary CTA → secondary CTA
userStories.notes: Per design principles §6.2 — this REPLACES the live site's 3-slide tagline-only rotator. The 3 slides become supporting visuals (rotating background photo) but the headline is now content-rich and stable.

userStories.title: Build ServiceTile component
userStories.description: As a designer, I need a ServiceTile for the homepage 5-up grid and the services index page.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/components/marketing/service-tile.tsx exports <ServiceTile {...props} />
Props: { number: string (e.g. '03'), name: string, lede: string, image: string, href: string, alt: string }
Layout: <Card> wrapping <Link href={href}>
Image: 16:10 aspect using next/image with explicit dimensions
Eyebrow label: 'Service {number} / {name}' (uppercase)
Title: <h3> font-text font-bold text-h4 (NOT uppercase per design principles §4.3)
Body: 2 lines of text-sm
Footer: arrow icon + 'Learn more' in text-pops-yellow-500
Hover: shadow-3 lift, image scale 1.03 over 200ms ease-out
Reduced-motion: hover scale disabled, shadow still applies
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tile renders, hover state works, keyboard focus shows ring
userStories.notes: Per design principles §6.3 — no tan/sepia tint on images (that's a current-site bug). Use real shop photography. The 5 services and their images are listed in PRD §7.

userStories.title: Build /(home) page with new hero + service grid + commitment columns
userStories.description: As a visitor, when I land on popsindustrial.com I see a content-rich hero, the 5-service grid, the commitment/facilities/standards columns, the family photo, and the footer.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/app/page.tsx exists and renders the home page

Hero uses /images/slide-01.jpg as background, eyebrow 'FAMILY OWNED · LAKELAND, FL · SINCE 1972', heading 'Four generations of industrial finishing — done right the first time.', lede '...' (see PRD §6.2 wireframe), primary CTA 'Request a Quote' → /request-a-quote, secondary CTA 'See our work' → /industrial-coatings-services
Below hero: section 'Four generations of expertise in industrial coatings' (H2)

5-up service grid using ServiceTile for: Wet Paint, Complex, Abrasive Media Blasting, Powder, Large Capacity Powder Coating (images from /images/{wet-paint-coatings,complex-coating,abrasive-media-blasting,powder-coating,large-capacity-powder-coating}-300x... per the live mapping in .firecrawl/popsindustrial/pages/home.md)
Three-column section: Commitment / Our Facilities & Equipment / Industry Standards & High Performance Solutions (content from .firecrawl/popsindustrial/pages/home.md)
Family photo section (use /images/pops-4-generations.jpg)
Footer renders below content
All images have meaningful alt text — NOT alt='' unless decorative
Exactly one <h1> on the page
Build succeeds; page renders at apps/pops-website dev server '/'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open / and visually compare against .firecrawl/popsindustrial/screenshots/desktop/home.png — all sections present
userStories.notes: Content source: .firecrawl/popsindustrial/pages/home.md (clean main content) + .firecrawl/popsindustrial/screenshots/desktop/home.png (visual reference). The hero is the BIGGEST DEPARTURE from the live site — read PRD §6.2 wireframe carefully. Keep the 4-generations / commitment / facilities / equipment / high-performance sections faithful to the existing copy.

Two cross-links: <Link href='/about-us/history'> 'Our History' and <Link href='/about-us/leadership'> 'Meet the Family'
Footer renders

4 <Card> components in a 2x2 grid (desktop) / 1-up stack (mobile)
Each card: full-res photo (/images/{chris,jamie,kaitlyn,marcus}-woods-320x480-1.jpg, NOT the -200x300 thumbnails), name, title, bio, LinkedIn link

userStories.title: Build /request-a-quote page (form scaffold)

Form using FormField + Input + Textarea + Button: name (required), email (required), phone, company, project details (textarea, required)

Form uses React Hook Form + Zod schema (Zod schema exported for reuse in server action)
All required fields show '*' in pops-yellow-500
Submit button: <Button variant='primary' size='default' type='submit'>
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tab order field-by-field, errors display when submitting empty
userStories.notes: Install react-hook-form, zod, @hookform/resolvers/zod into apps/pops-website/. The Zod schema goes in apps/pops-website/app/request-a-quote/schema.ts so the server action (US-036) imports the same source. Form layout per design principles §6.4.

userStories.title: Build /industrial-coatings-services index page

userStories.title: Build sendFormEmail() Resend helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one shared server-side helper that takes (toAddress, subject, htmlBody) and dispatches a transactional email via Resend so all four form server actions reuse it.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/lib/email.ts exports async function sendFormEmail({ to, subject, html, replyTo? })
Reads RESEND_API_KEY from process.env (throws if missing)
Uses 'resend' npm package (add to apps/pops-website/package.json)
From-address is configurable via RESEND_FROM env var with a fallback to 'noreply@popsindustrial.com'
Returns the Resend response or throws a typed error
Includes a basic HTML email template helper that wraps form fields in a readable table
Logs failures to Sentry via @sentry/nextjs (Sentry is already in root package.json)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read the RESEND env names in CLAUDE.md or root .env.example if available. Don't accidentally use a different SMTP provider — the project locks to Resend.

Validates input; returns { ok: false, errors } on validation failure
On valid input, calls sendFormEmail({ to: company.emails.info, subject: `New quote request from ${name}`, html: <renders form fields> })
Returns { ok: true } on success
request-a-quote/page.tsx (from US-021) wired to call submitQuote and render success state inline
On submit, button shows loading state (per US-008 button loading); on result, form clears or shows server-side error
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: submit a valid form, see success message; submit invalid, see field-level errors
userStories.notes: Server actions in Next.js 16 are async functions exported from a 'use server' module. Per CLAUDE.md, Server Actions are the preferred mutation path. reCAPTCHA verification gets bolted on in US-039.

userStories.title: Wire Google reCAPTCHA v3 into all 4 form actions
userStories.description: As an operator, I need bot protection on every public form so we don't get spam quote requests or check-ins.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/lib/recaptcha.ts exports async function verifyRecaptcha(token: string, action: string): Promise<{ success: boolean, score: number }>
Calls https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api/siteverify with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY
Threshold: score >= 0.5 to pass; below = treat as bot
All 4 server actions (submitQuote, submitContact, submitCheckIn, submitCheckOut) call verifyRecaptcha before sendFormEmail
Client side: each form page loads the reCAPTCHA v3 script via next/script with NEXT_PUBLIC_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY env var
Each form requests a fresh token on submit and includes it in formData
Failed reCAPTCHA returns { ok: false, errors: { _form: 'reCAPTCHA verification failed. Please try again.' } } from the action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: form submits when verification passes (visible reCAPTCHA badge bottom-right)
userStories.notes: reCAPTCHA v3 is invisible (v2 is the checkbox; v3 is score-based). Per scrape, the live site already uses v3 — keep the same UX. Document the two env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY, RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY) in apps/pops-website/.env.example.

acceptanceCriteria: Run a script (or grep) across apps/pops-website/app/**/*.tsx and components/**/*.tsx finding every and

Decorative images (background-only, purely visual) explicitly have alt=''
Content images have descriptive alt — at minimum 'Industrial powder coating equipment at Pop's Lakeland facility' style descriptions
apps/pops-website/scripts/audit-alt.ts (or similar) exists and prints PASS/FAIL — wire into pnpm lint script
Updated apps/pops-website/package.json#scripts.lint chains the alt audit
All existing pages pass the audit
Typecheck passes

userStories.notes: This fixes a real live-site accessibility gap (17/18 pages have zero alt) per .firecrawl/popsindustrial/_raw/site-globals.json#alt_text_audit. The rebuild is the chance to do this right.

userStories.title: Heading-order audit + skip-to-content verification
userStories.description: As a screen-reader user, every page has exactly one H1 and a monotonic heading order so I can navigate by headings.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/scripts/audit-headings.ts walks every app/**/page.tsx, parses the JSX, and verifies: exactly one <h1>, no skipped heading levels (no h2 → h4)
Audit fails build if violations found
Skip-to-content link (from US-014 Header) is the first focusable element on every page
Add an a11y.test.tsx Playwright/Vitest test (if test infrastructure exists in apps/pops-website — otherwise document in progress.txt that test infra is deferred to a follow-up)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tab through / and confirm first focusable element is skip-to-content
userStories.notes: If parsing JSX is too involved for one Ralph iteration, write a simpler grep-based audit that counts <h1> occurrences per page.tsx — single-file pages should have at most one h1 reference.

userStories.title: Playwright contrast lint — fail build on AA violations
userStories.description: As a designer, the build refuses to ship if any rendered page has a color combination below WCAG AA (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large).
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/scripts/contrast-lint.ts runs Playwright against the dev server
Visits all 18 pages; for each, computes contrast for every text/background pair using axe-core or @axe-core/playwright
Reports any pair with contrast ratio < 4.5 (normal) or < 3.0 (large >24px or 18px-bold)
Exits non-zero if any violations
Wire into apps/pops-website/package.json#scripts.contrast-lint
Add @axe-core/playwright to apps/pops-website devDependencies
Run reports zero failures (because tokens are designed to pass — see design principles §3.5)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Per design principles §3.5, every token pair in the system passes AA. If this lint surfaces a violation, it's a regression in implementation, not the tokens. Run as part of CI.

userStories.title: Lighthouse CI budgets
userStories.description: As an operator, every PR runs Lighthouse on the deployed preview and fails if Performance < 95 mobile or < 98 desktop on home page.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/lighthouserc.json (or .lighthouseci/) configured with mobile + desktop assertions
Performance ≥ 95 mobile, ≥ 98 desktop
Best Practices ≥ 95
Accessibility = 100
SEO ≥ 95
Run via pnpm dlx @lhci/cli autorun against http://localhost:3000 (after build + start)
If Vercel preview is configured, Lighthouse can run against the preview URL — note the env var pattern
Wire into apps/pops-website/package.json#scripts.lighthouse
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Static marketing site → these budgets are achievable. If Lighthouse fails, identify which budget is breached and address (e.g., image preload, font preconnect, smaller hero image) — but in this story just SET the budgets, don't over-tune.

userStories.title: Mobile responsive audit + fixes
userStories.description: As a mobile visitor at 375px viewport, every page renders without horizontal scroll, with touch targets ≥ 44px and readable text.
acceptanceCriteria: Playwright script visits all 18 pages at 375x844 viewport (iPhone 14 Pro)
Asserts no horizontal overflow (scrollWidth === clientWidth on <body>)
Asserts every interactive element (button, a, input) has bounding box >= 44px in at least one dimension
Asserts body text computed font-size >= 14px on mobile
Compare each new screenshot to .firecrawl/popsindustrial/screenshots/mobile/<slug>.png — note major content omissions in progress.txt (this is the parity check)
Fix any violations found; re-run until clean
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: at 375w, no horizontal scroll on any page
userStories.notes: Reference screenshots: .firecrawl/popsindustrial/screenshots/mobile/. The new site doesn't need to look identical — the new design is a deliberate improvement — but content parity (every section that existed before still exists somewhere) must hold.

userStories.title: Vercel deploy config (vercel.ts) + domain setup docs
userStories.description: As an operator, I can deploy apps/pops-website to Vercel and have it serve popsindustrial.com.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/vercel.ts exports a config matching the design (per Vercel guidance, vercel.ts is preferred over vercel.json)
Config sets framework: 'nextjs', buildCommand: 'pnpm build', installCommand: 'pnpm install --filter pops-website... --frozen-lockfile'
Cron entries: none for marketing site
Headers entry adds Cache-Control public, max-age=1 week, immutable for /images/* and /pdfs/*
Headers entry adds X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff and Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin to all routes
apps/pops-website/.env.example committed listing: RESEND_API_KEY, RESEND_FROM, RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY, SENTRY_DSN, NEXT_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN

apps/pops-website/README.md includes 'Deploy to Vercel' section: link Vercel project to apps/pops-website as the project root, set env vars listed above, add domain popsindustrial.com (and www → 301 → apex)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Per Vercel knowledge update: vercel.ts (typed config via @vercel/config) is the modern way. Install @vercel/config in apps/pops-website. The actual Vercel project linking is a manual operator step — document it, don't try to do it from Ralph.

userStories.title: apps/pops-website/README.md handoff documentation
userStories.description: As the next person to work on this site, I have a single README that explains how to run, build, deploy, and where the design + content sources live.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/pops-website/README.md exists with sections: Overview, Quickstart (dev), Project structure, Design system reference, Content sources, Forms (env vars + Resend setup), Deploy (Vercel), Troubleshooting
Quickstart shows: pnpm install, pnpm --filter pops-website dev, opens http://localhost:3000
Design system reference section links to docs/design/popsindustrial-design-principles.md
Content sources section links to .firecrawl/popsindustrial/manifest.json and explains how to refresh
Forms section enumerates env vars from US-039 + US-035 + US-048
Deploy section is the same as US-048 README block
Troubleshooting covers: fonts not loading (next/font cache), Resend 401 (RESEND_API_KEY missing), reCAPTCHA failing (NEXT_PUBLIC_RECAPTCHA_SITE_KEY mismatch with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the LAST story. By the time Ralph reaches it, every other piece exists. The README is documentation only — make it accurate, not aspirational.
